answer:
The standard error for autocorrelation coefficients under the null hypothesis of no autocorrelation is approximately \(1/\sqrt{T}\), where \(T\) is the sample size. With 250 data points, the standard error is about \(1/\sqrt{250} \approx 0.0632\). At the 5% significance level, the critical value is roughly 1.96, so coefficients greater than \(0.0632 \times 1.96 \approx 0.124\) in absolute value are significant; only the lag 1 coefficient of 0.2 exceeds this threshold.  

\boxed{E}
